---
title: "Publications & research"
---

Peer-reviewed work, preprints, and the thesis. This list is kept current; work in
progress is marked as such.

## Peer-reviewed

- **The Impact of COVID-19 on Globalization.** *One Health* 11:100180 (2020).
  [doi:10.1016/j.onehlt.2020.100180](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.onehlt.2020.100180).
  Contributed quantitative analysis of the relationships between pandemic spread,
  mobility patterns, and global economic disruption.

<!-- Template — copy per entry:
- Author(s). **Title.** *Journal* vol:pages (year). [doi:…](https://doi.org/…)
-->

## Preprints

- **Spatial Patterns of COVID-19 Mortality: Examining Socioeconomic Determinants
  in U.S. Counties Using Cluster Analysis.** Wardrup R. medRxiv (2024).
  Sole-author study using cluster analysis to identify spatial patterns in
  U.S. county-level COVID-19 mortality and the socioeconomic determinants
  associated with them.
  <!-- Add the medRxiv DOI/URL here once available. -->

## Thesis

- **Bayesian spatiotemporal modeling of West Nile virus in California:
  environmental drivers, surveillance effort, and mosquito-pool infection.**
  MPH thesis, University of Memphis. In progress; expected 2027.
  [Project page](projects/wnv-thesis.qmd)

## Unpublished manuscripts

- **Spatio-temporal clustering of Lyme disease in Texas, 2000–2014.**
  Wardrup RR. Unpublished manuscript, 2015. A sole-author study across all 254
  Texas counties using SaTScan space-time cluster detection and Kendall's rank
  correlation to test association with deciduous-forest habitat; detected one
  significant space-time cluster in southeastern Texas and found no significant
  forest-habitat association. Early independent research; submitted but not
  published.

## Talks & presentations

*None yet listed.*

<!--
- **Title.** Event, location, month year. [Slides](…)
-->

## Posters

*None yet listed.*

<!--
- Author(s). **Title.** Conference, year. [Poster (PDF)](…)
-->

---

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Full author lists, DOIs, and slide/poster links are added to each entry as they
become available.
:::
